answer.text: The linear relationship between bond price and yield
The degree of the curve in the relationship between bond prices and bond yields
The credit risk of the bond issuer
The reinvestment risk of coupon payments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Convexity measures the degree of curvature in the price-yield relationship. Since the relationship is non-linear, duration alone (which approximates a linear change) is insufficient for large yield changes. Convexity improves the accuracy of price change estimates. **The Trap:** Duration measures the slope (first derivative), while convexity measures the curvature (second derivative) of the price-yield curve. **Cross-Exam Context:** Convexity is tested in X-A and IV (Interest Rate Derivatives) for advanced bond analytics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Higher convexity is always desirable — a bond with higher convexity will gain more when yields fall and lose less when yields rise, all else being equal.
